name: codex-mcp-doctor
description: Diagnose MCP server connectivity, configuration correctness, runtime health, tool schema quality, security risks (prompt injection, tool shadowing, hidden Unicode), supply-chain version pinning, plaintext secrets, latency thresholds, and rug-pull detection (tool-description baseline). Zero external dependencies - pure Python 3.11+ stdlib.

interface.shortDescription: Diagnose and fix MCP server issues
interface.longDescription: Automatically detects MCP configuration problems, connectivity failures, missing tools, broken tool schemas, security risks (prompt injection, tool poisoning, hidden Unicode), supply-chain version pinning issues, plaintext secrets in config, elevated latency, and rug-pull attacks (tool-description changes). Runs zero-dependency diagnostics on stdio and HTTP/SSE MCP servers, checking all three primitives (tools, resources, prompts). Reports root causes with fix suggestions, health scores, schema quality issues, and multi-layer security analysis.
defaultPrompt: Run mcp-doctor to check my MCP servers
My MCP tools are missing, diagnose why
Check if all my MCP servers are healthy
Scan my MCP servers for security risks
Check for prompt injection in my MCP tools
Check if my MCP tools changed since last baseline (rug-pull)
Scan my config for plaintext secrets and unpinned packages

screenshots.caption: Real diagnostics on a 3-server Codex setup: health scores, schema issues, prompt-injection (W001), destructive-capability (W019), sensitive-data (W017), plaintext-secret detection
E003 Rug-Pull Detection: high-severity tool-description change, medium new-tool-since-baseline, low tool-removed-since-baseline
